name: Yotpo
what_it_does: Yotpo is an ecommerce retention marketing platform providing APIs for collecting and displaying customer reviews, ratings, photos/video UGC, loyalty and referral programs, and SMS marketing.
use_cases: Agents requesting reviews from customers post-purchase via automated email or SMS review request flows
Fetching review data and ratings to populate product pages or feed into recommendation engines
Managing loyalty points and rewards — agents crediting or redeeming points on behalf of customers
Syndicating UGC (photos, videos, Q&A) to storefront widgets or social channels programmatically
Reporting pipelines pulling aggregate review scores and sentiment for product quality dashboards
not_for: B2B software review collection — G2 or Trustpilot are better suited for software product reviews
Enterprise-scale brand reputation management across non-ecommerce channels — use Bazaarvoice or Sprinklr
Lightweight review widgets with minimal setup — Stamped.io or Judge.me are simpler and cheaper
best_when: You're running a DTC or ecommerce brand on Shopify or BigCommerce and need tightly integrated reviews, loyalty, and SMS in one platform with a single API surface.
avoid_when: You only need basic star ratings without loyalty or SMS — standalone review tools will be cheaper and simpler to integrate.

auth.notes: App key and secret for server-side calls; OAuth2 for partner integrations. Separate API keys per product (Reviews, Loyalty, SMSBump). Key management is fragmented across product lines.

pricing.notes: Pricing is highly opaque — most plans require a sales conversation. Reviews has a starter tier but Loyalty and SMS are enterprise-priced. Total cost scales with order volume and contacts.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by plan; typically 100–500 req/min for REST endpoints

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Documentation is scattered across product lines (Reviews, Loyalty, SMSBump have separate docs). Error responses are inconsistent — some endpoints return plain strings, others return structured JSON errors.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Review creation endpoints accept external IDs for deduplication. Loyalty point transactions are not idempotent by default.
known_agent_gotchas: Multiple separate API keys across product lines — Reviews, Loyalty, and SMSBump each have their own credentials
Rate limits are not clearly published and vary by plan — agents may hit undocumented limits
Domain/store verification required before API access is active
Webhook payloads differ significantly across product lines — implement per-product handlers
Free tier does not exist — requires paid plan before any API calls work

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II certified. GDPR and CCPA compliant. TLS enforced. OAuth2 available for partner apps. Multiple API keys across product lines increases secret sprawl risk.
